channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson Wed in West Palm Beach
item.description: President Trump’s eldest son was married in Palm Beach, Fla. The president described celebrations this weekend as a “small, little private affair.”
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan and Tammy LaGorce
item.media:description: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson were legally married on Thursday in West Palm Beach, Fla., according to Palm Beach County marriage records.

item.title: Cannes Film Festival 2026: All the Red Carpet Photos
item.description: Photos from the South of France, where Ruth Negga, Sharon Stone, John Travolta and more went all out for the first week of the annual film festival.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Scott A Garfitt/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Ruth Negga at the Cannes Film Festival.

item.title: Tom Sachs Wants to Move On
item.description: The notorious MacGyver of the art world, who weathered a scandal three years ago, has re-emerged with a big new show.
item.dc:creator: Ruth La Ferla and Evelyn Freja
item.media:description: Tom Sachs’s deliberately rough-hewed looking lamps, ceramics and furniture on view through June 20 at Salon 94 in Manhattan.

item.title: Jenna Lyons Has Thoughts on What You Should Wear to That Wedding
item.description: For starters, don’t confuse a wedding with a workday.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Benveniste
item.media:credit: Natalie Keyssar for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I feel like at some point, in a previous life, I was a peacock or a bowerbird. I want to bring it.”

item.title: ‘Whimsy,’ a New Trend, May Be a Life Raft for Gen-Z and Millennials
item.description: To a younger generation, the word describes a playful response to a difficult world — even if it might strike some as a precious form of capitalism.
item.dc:creator: Brian Josephs

item.title: In Musk vs. OpenAI, the Real Winner Was the Suit
item.description: It was the one thing on which every major player could agree.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for the New York Times, Josh Edelson/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: At RuPaul’s ‘Stop! That! Train!’ Premiere, Drag Queens and Gay Icons Served ‘Choo-Choo Realness’
item.description: In Los Angeles, at the premiere of “Stop! That! Train!” a disaster film parody set on the runaway Glamazonian Express, the dress code was “choo-choo realness.”
item.dc:creator: Juno Carmel
item.media:description: RuPaul, center, at the hot pink carpet premiere of “Stop! That! Train!” with alumni of “RuPaul’s Drag Race.”

item.title: Gucci Got Tom Brady and Cindy Crawford to Strut Their Stuff in Times Square
item.description: And other memorable moments, and clothes, from Demna’s first cruise show.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: These Designers Want to Dress Your Bed
item.description: Millaux sheets are made to order. But the designers don’t want the pillowcases to match. Ever.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale
item.media:credit: Yael Malka for The New York Times

item.title: The Case for Ugly-Cool Sunglasses
item.description: Shiny, wraparound, functional — Oakleys at the French Open. Plus other tennis style moments and more.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: A Harlem Doyenne’s Head-Turning Dress
item.description: A Harlem doyenne with a fondness for “a 1950s silhouette” took matters into her own hands and merged two skirts into one head-turning dress.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: The Man Behind the People Gallery Wants to Hear About Your Fit
item.description: Maurice Kamara has a gift for getting people to stop for his man-on-the-street style interviews. That includes A-listers who usually couldn’t be bothered.
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia and Aundre Larrow

item.title: Is the Handbag Over?
item.description: A reader has noticed a different kind of trend.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: ‘Debbie Downer’ Is Enjoying an Upswing
item.description: With her popular mystical podcast, a return to Broadway in “The Rocky Horror Show” and a second Tony nomination, Rachel Dratch is finally getting her due.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Pilat
item.media:credit: OK McCausland for The New York Times

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson Celebrate Marriage on Bahamas Private Island
item.description: After getting legally married in Palm Beach, the couple exchanged vows in the Caribbean this weekend, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson in Palm Beach, Fla., in February.

item.title: No Diamond Ring? Put a String on It.
item.description: Proposals, whether on-the-spot or well thought out, can occur without a traditional ring, and sometimes with just words alone.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: Youn Yuh-jung of “Beef” on the Tiffany Necklace She Loved, Lost and Replaced
item.description: After a robbery, the Oscar-winning actress, now starring in the second season of “Beef,” replaced the heart-shaped necklace she adored.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Diamond
item.media:credit: Justin Chung for The New York Times

item.title: Unforgettable Red Carpet Looks at Cannes: Demi Moore, John Travolta and More
item.description: From John Travolta’s beret to Barbara Palvin’s elegant pregnancy style, the red carpets from the festival in France had many memorable moments.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi, Vanessa Friedman and Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: Barbara Palvin in Miu Miu with Dylan Sprouse.

item.title: John Travolta Pulled Off a Beret. Could You?
item.description: Plus: Louis Vuitton’s cruise show at the Frick and a smoked-fish deli makes merch.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: Et voilà: John Travolta at Cannes, a hat guy now.

item.title: Everlane Finalizes Sale to Shein, the Fast Fashion Giant
item.description: The ultra low priced retailer has purchased Everlane, the millennial standby that promised transparency.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Justin Kaneps for The New York Times
item.media:description: Founded in 2011, Everlane was positioned as a environmentally conscious label.

item.title: Stephen Colbert Suited Himself
item.description: The suit may be a totem of conventionality, but for late-night hosts, it has become a useful tool.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: Stephen Colbert during the “Late Show” finale on Thursday.

item.title: The Miles Davis Look: His Sunglasses, Trumpet, Hair and More
item.description: Fast cars, huge shades and, surprisingly, any old trumpet: These are the things that made Miles Miles.
item.dc:creator: Louis Lucero II
item.media:credit: From left; Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images; Christian Rose/Roger Viollet, via Getty Images; Mark Patiky/Condé Nast, via Getty Images; David Redfern/Redferns

item.title: They Quarantined Together in London, Then Married in New York
item.description: After an early trip together turned into a quarantine during a record heat wave, Lila Newman and Lency Cantú blended New York and Tex-Mex traditions at their Hotel Chelsea wedding.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: After Years of Near-Misses, a Match That Felt ‘Like Divine Timing’
item.description: Marissa Anto and Kevin Anthony grew up near each other and went to school in the same city at the same time, yet it took Bumble to connect.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Men, Hide Your Cats
item.description: Or at least keep them out of your online dating profile pics.
item.dc:creator: Michael Zadoorian

item.title: She Was Covering President Biden. He Was Protecting Him. Love Took Flight.
item.description: Mariam Khan, a producer for ABC News, was in St. Croix covering the former president’s vacation when Phillip McCoy, a fighter pilot, spotted her on the beach.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: The Sweetest Ceremony — at Their Favorite Ice Cream Shop
item.description: Dr. Michelle Nguyen and Alex Dias got married among scoopers and freezers at Salt & Straw in Portland, Ore.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: Valie Export, Who Made Art With Her Naked Body, Dies at 85
item.description: In provocative performance pieces and films, she forced viewers to confront misogyny in the media and society at large.
item.dc:creator: Will Heinrich
item.media:description: Ms. Export at the opening of a retrospective of her work at the Albertina in Vienna in 2023. What distinguished her art, beyond its aggression and sheer volume, was its lucidity.

item.title: Everything We Know About ‘The White Lotus’ Season 4
item.description: Where is the latest batch of guests jetting off to? What stars will be lighting up the screen? Here’s what to know ahead of the latest season.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy
item.media:description: The Hotel Martinez, an Art Deco building that will be transformed into the White Lotus Cannes.

item.title: Louis Vuitton Gets Weird at the Frick
item.description: Keith Haring met the Gilded Age in LV’s cruise show.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: A Keith Haring-inspired jacket in the Louis Vuitton cruise show at the Frick.

item.title: Adidas’s ‘Endurance Suit’ Makes Runners Faster. But Will They Wear It?
item.description: A state-of-the-art “Endurance Suit” can enhance marathon performance and stave off the effects of fatigue — if only Adidas can convince its top athletes to wear it.
item.dc:creator: Calum Marsh

item.title: Why Can’t I Leave My Cheating Husband?
item.description: Our Ask the Therapist columnist, Lori Gottlieb, advises a reader who feels stuck despite a series of infidelities.
item.dc:creator: Lori Gottlieb

item.title: Hooters Says Bring the Kids
item.description: The chain known for skimpy uniforms and “bikini nights” is trying to change its image. Hooters onesie, anyone?
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Schade and Sydney Krantz
item.media:description: A recent gathering of a youth baseball team at the Hooters in East Brunswick, N.J.

item.title: You Can Give Knicks Fans T-Shirts. But Will They Wear Them? Well …
item.description: In arenas all across the league, N.B.A. fans embrace the monochrome T-shirt as a way to show team spirit. New Yorkers aren’t having it.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Keh

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Delight to Your Day
item.description: Travel cake, telegrams and a possibly addictive word game.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘A Misdirected Midlife Crisis’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: Can I Ask My Parents to Put Away Their Phones When They Watch My Children?
item.description: A reader dreads the thought of chiding his parents for their distracted babysitting, but he has already noticed the potential risks of their inattention.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: ‘Modern Love’: Escape Rooms as the Cure for Heartbreak
item.description: Faced with a sudden end to her marriage, Lauren Bans found relief in an unexpected place.
item.dc:creator: Anna Martin, Reva Goldberg, Davis Land, Sara Curtis, Elisa Gutierrez, Lynn Levy, Daniel Ramirez, Dan Powell, Pat McCusker and Elisheba Ittoop
